controls.label: Does your business have a Beneficial Ownership process?
controls.remediation: Your organisation does not have a Beneficial Ownership process in place. You must establish a documented Beneficial Ownership process to meet your AML/CTF obligations. Review the controls below and implement the required procedures before completing this assessment.

controls.label: Does your Beneficial Ownership procedure collect the beneficial owner's full name, and either date of birth or full residential address, before or as soon as practicable after providing designated services?
controls.detail-label: Describe your collection process and timing
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification procedure does not collect the minimum required information for beneficial owners. You must collect each beneficial owner's full name and either their date of birth or full residential address before providing designated services (or as soon as practicable after), as required under rules 4.12.1, 4.12.1(1), 4.12.1(1)(a), 4.12.1(1)(b), and 4.12.1(1)(c).

controls.label: For individual customers, does your procedure assume the customer and beneficial owner are the same person unless you have reasonable grounds to consider otherwise?
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification procedure does not correctly handle individual customers. When the customer is an individual, you may assume they are the beneficial owner unless you have reasonable grounds to consider otherwise, as permitted under rule 4.12.2(1).

controls.label: Does your procedure exempt customers from beneficial ownership collection when they are verified under simplified procedures, are Australian Government Entities, or are foreign listed public companies with transparency requirements?
controls.detail-label: Describe which exemptions your procedure applies
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification procedure does not apply the permitted exemptions. You are not required to collect beneficial ownership information for customers verified under simplified company procedures (4.3.8), simplified trustee procedures (4.4.8), Australian Government Entities, or foreign listed public companies with disclosure requirements, as provided under rules 4.12.2(2)(a) through 4.12.2(2)(e).

checklist-items.label: Foreign listed public company with disclosure requirements

controls.label: Does your procedure include risk-based systems to determine whether additional information about beneficial owners should be collected and verified beyond the minimum requirements?
controls.detail-label: Describe your risk-based approach to additional beneficial owner information
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification procedure lacks risk-based systems to assess whether additional beneficial owner information should be collected. You must implement appropriate risk-based controls to determine when to collect and verify information beyond the minimum requirements, as required under rule 4.12.3.

controls.label: Does your procedure require verification of beneficial owner information using reliable and independent documentation, electronic data, or a combination of both?
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification procedure does not require proper verification of beneficial owner information. Verification must be based on reliable and independent documentation, reliable and independent electronic data, or a combination of both, as required under rules 4.12.4, 4.12.4(1), 4.12.4(2), and 4.12.4(3).

items.label: Combination of documentation and electronic data

controls.label: For medium or lower ML/TF risk beneficial owners, do you use the safe harbour verification procedure (and confirm no beneficial owner is a foreign politically exposed person)?
controls.detail-label: Describe when and how you apply the safe harbour procedure
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification procedure does not utilize the safe harbour verification procedure for medium or lower ML/TF risk beneficial owners. You may streamline verification using the safe harbour procedure outlined in rule 4.12.7, provided no beneficial owner is a foreign politically exposed person, as specified in rules 4.12.5 and 4.12.6.

controls.label: If using safe harbour, does your procedure collect all information described in 4.12.1 for each beneficial owner?
controls.remediation: Your safe harbour Beneficial Ownership procedure does not collect the required minimum information. Even when using safe harbour verification, you must still collect each beneficial owner's full name and either date of birth or full residential address, as required under rule 4.12.7(1).

controls.label: If using documentation-based safe harbour, does your procedure verify beneficial owner name and either address or date of birth using either: (a) primary photographic ID, or (b) primary non-photographic ID plus secondary ID?
controls.remediation: Your documentation-based safe harbour procedure does not use the correct identification documents. You must verify beneficial owner name and either residential address or date of birth using an original or certified copy of: (a) primary photographic ID, or (b) both primary non-photographic ID and secondary ID, as required under rules 4.12.7(2), 4.12.7(2)(a), 4.12.7(2)(b), 4.12.7(2)(i), and 4.12.7(2)(i)(ii).

items.label: Primary photographic identification document (original or certified copy)
Primary non-photographic ID + secondary ID (both original or certified copies)

controls.label: If using documentation-based safe harbour, does your procedure verify that documents have not expired (except Commonwealth passports expired within two years)?
controls.remediation: Your documentation-based safe harbour procedure does not verify document expiry. You must confirm that documents produced for beneficial owner verification have not expired, with the exception of Commonwealth passports that expired within the preceding two years, as required under rule 4.12.7(3).

controls.label: If using electronic-based safe harbour, does your procedure verify beneficial owner name and either address or date of birth using reliable and independent electronic data from at least two separate data sources?
controls.detail-label: Describe your electronic data sources and verification process
controls.remediation: Your electronic-based safe harbour procedure does not use sufficient data sources. You must verify beneficial owner name and either residential address or date of birth using reliable and independent electronic data from at least two separate data sources, as required under rule 4.12.7(4).

checklist-items.label: At least two separate data sources

controls.label: Does your procedure include risk-based systems to respond to discrepancies in beneficial owner verification so you can be reasonably satisfied the beneficial owner is who the customer claims?
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification procedure lacks appropriate controls for handling verification discrepancies. You must implement risk-based systems to respond to any discrepancies that arise during verification, enabling you to determine that you are reasonably satisfied the beneficial owner is the person the customer claims, as required under rule 4.12.8.

controls.label: If you cannot ascertain a beneficial owner, does your procedure require identification and reasonable verification of alternative control persons (e.g., 25%+ voting rights holders or senior managing officials)?
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification procedure does not have adequate fallback controls when the beneficial owner cannot be ascertained. You must identify and take reasonable measures to verify alternative control persons such as individuals with 25% or more voting rights, senior managing officials, or those with power to appoint/remove trustees, as required under rules 4.12.9, 4.12.9(1), 4.12.9(2), and 4.12.9(3).

checklist-items.label: For companies/partnerships: individuals with 25%+ voting rights or senior managing officials
For trusts: individuals who can appoint or remove trustees
For associations/co-operatives: individuals with 25%+ voting rights, 25%+ dissolution entitlement, or senior managing officials

groups.title: Process Confirmation
groups.description: Before answering the detailed compliance questions, confirm whether your organisation operates this business process.

groups.title: Collection of Beneficial Owner Information
groups.description: Determine and collect minimum required information about each beneficial owner before or as soon as practicable after providing designated services.

groups.title: Beneficial Ownership Exemptions
groups.description: Apply permitted exemptions for specific customer types that do not require beneficial ownership collection.

groups.title: Risk-Based Additional Collection
groups.description: Assess whether additional beneficial owner information beyond the minimum should be collected and verified based on ML/TF risk.

groups.title: Verification of Beneficial Owner Information
groups.description: Verify collected beneficial owner information using reliable and independent sources.

groups.title: Safe Harbour Verification Procedure
groups.description: Optional streamlined verification procedure for medium or lower ML/TF risk beneficial owners (not applicable if any beneficial owner is a foreign politically exposed person).

groups.title: Responding to Verification Discrepancies
groups.description: Handle any discrepancies that arise during beneficial owner verification to achieve reasonable satisfaction of identity.

groups.title: Fallback Procedure When Beneficial Owner Cannot Be Ascertained
groups.description: Alternative identification requirements when you are unable to determine the beneficial owner.
